sections.imageAlt: Colorful umbrellas symbolizing diverse market perspectives
sections.title: Close the Intention-Action Gap
sections.subtitle: If your investments in sustainability and social impact aren't getting any traction, we can help you find out why.

sections.heading: Closing the Gaps: Transforming Purpose into Profit
paragraphs: Most brands and businesses want to do the right thing. But that doesn't always mean they're doing the right things. Sometimes those good intentions just aren't landing. You can see the big picture (and the opportunity) so clearly, but teams aren't aligned, the business isn't buying it and leadership won't support it — despite all the data.
Sound familiar? If so, then the chances are there's an intention-action gap. We can help you find it, quantify it and close it.

left.alt: 4C landscape assessment diagram showing culture, category, consumer, and competitor gaps

right.body:
## Finding the Gap

We'll start by undertaking a complimentary AI driven **5C landscape assessment** (company, culture, category, consumer and competitor) to identify where your biggest intention action gaps are — inside and out.

**The result:** An initial assessment of the intention action gaps preventing your brand or business from commercializing sustainability — along with a strategic recommendation on how to close the biggest gap — quantifying that lost opportunity.

left.body:
## Mapping the Ecosystem

Once the biggest gap has been identified, we go deeper. Using **interviews, workshops and ethnographic research** we map out the ecosystem that connects all of your key stakeholders — capturing their needs, drivers, expectations and pain points — bringing the human insight and nuance that machines will always miss.

**The result:** A clear map of the most significant drivers, barriers, pain points and bottle-necks that are influencing behavior across your stakeholder's ecosystem.

right.alt: Ecosystem mapping visualization showing stakeholder connections and influence networks

left.alt: Quantification chart showing implicit association testing results and barrier-driver analysis

right.body:
## Right to Win

Insight without ROI doesn't get buy-in. In the final stage, we deploy **Implicit Association Testing (IAT)** to measure the unconscious barriers and drivers behind your team culture and consumer behaviour — connecting the dots. This isn't opinion polling — it's the very latest in neuroscience and behavioral research!

We isolate the factors that actually determine whether people will act and then wrap it all up into a propelling problem that your brand or business is uniquely positioned to solve. And, we'll also show you what you can own and how that differentiates you from the competition in your category.

items.title: Data Dashboard & Report
items.body: Qualitative and quantitative data with confidence levels that you can prioritize and adjust to identify your biggest intention-action gap.

items.title: Experience Map
items.body: An overview of the barriers, drivers, pressure points and bottlenecks that characterize your key stakeholders and influence their behavior.

items.title: Team Collaboration & Buy-In
items.body: A clear articulation of the problem to solve, path to alignment, right to win and size of the prize.

sections.newsletterSubtext: Get the latest articles, research, reports, case studies and insights on closing the intention-action gap direct to your inbox twice a month.
sections.bookingSubtext: Book a discovery call and we'll get started on your landscape assessment. In the meantime if you have any questions or want to get a sense for what your biggest intention-action gaps might be — Ask Gaia.

sections.sectionSubtitle: Everything you need to know about our market research process.

items.question: What does the 4C Landscape Assessment include?
items.answer: The assessment covers four dimensions: Culture (internal values and external societal trends), Category (market dynamics and emerging opportunities), Consumer (audience behaviours, needs, and unmet demands), and Competitor (positioning, messaging, and whitespace analysis). It's a comprehensive strategic foundation, not a surface-level audit.

items.question: How do you identify the intention-action gap?
items.answer: We combine qualitative research (interviews, ethnographies, focus groups) with quantitative validation (Implicit Association Testing). This dual approach reveals not just what people say they'll do, but what actually drives — or blocks — their behaviour. The gap between stated intention and real action is where the biggest opportunities live.

items.question: Is the research AI-led or human-led?
items.answer: Both. Our AI tool Gaia accelerates qualitative data synthesis — processing interview transcripts, identifying patterns across large datasets, and flagging emerging themes at speed. But every insight is validated and contextualised by our research team. AI handles volume; humans handle nuance.

items.question: How are stakeholders involved in the process?
items.answer: Stakeholder involvement is central to Stage 2. We design and facilitate interviews and focus groups with key internal and external stakeholders — from C-suite leadership to frontline teams to external partners. This ensures the research reflects real-world dynamics, not just assumptions.

items.question: What's the typical timeline?
items.answer: Most engagements run 2 to 6 weeks depending on scope. A single-market sprint can be completed in as little as 2 weeks. Multi-market or enterprise-scale projects with extensive stakeholder mapping typically take 4 to 6 weeks. We'll scope the timeline precisely during your discovery call.

items.question: Do you benchmark against competitors?
items.answer: Yes. Competitor analysis is a core part of the 4C assessment. We go beyond surface-level comparisons — we analyse messaging, positioning, sustainability claims, audience perception, and whitespace opportunities. The goal is to find where your brand can credibly differentiate.

items.question: Can this work for a single market or does it need to be enterprise-scale?
items.answer: Both. Our methodology scales to fit. We work with startups validating a single market entry through to global enterprises mapping stakeholder ecosystems across multiple regions. The framework is the same; the depth and breadth flex to match your needs.

items.question: How is Implicit Association Testing (IAT) validated?
items.answer: IAT is a well-established behavioural science methodology used in academic research for decades. We apply it in a commercial context to measure unconscious associations and biases that traditional surveys miss. Results are statistically validated and triangulated against our qualitative findings for maximum reliability.
